name: Hikma Health Inc
mission: We partner with nonprofit organizations providing healthcare in underserved and refugee populations. We create customized data systems for these healthc...
theoryOfChange: Hikma Health partners with nonprofit organizations providing healthcare in underserved and refugee populations to create customized data systems and offer clinicians data-driven tools. By making healthcare data accessible, secure, and actionable (including enabling patients to access/port their records and enabling clinicians to access authorized patient records across clinics), they aim to improve patient outcomes and support personalized, continuous care.

grantsData.purpose: Grant to Parkers Mobile Clinic, a non profit in Nigeria providing healthcare services to deploy the Hikma Health EHR system.

scoreSummary: HIKMA HEALTH INC shows strong alignment and good impact (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions high financial health and a 100% program expense ratio, but doesn't address the very low cost per beneficiary ($0.06) which is flagged as a medium severity risk and significantly impacts the impact score. This omission makes the rationale incomplete.

The rationale doesn't explicitly mention the risk deductions applied due to the high domestic burn rate and the implausibly low cost per beneficiary. While the overall amal_score rationale mentions the impact score being 'tempered,' it doesn't specify the risk factors contributing to this.
Website citation for 'beneficiaries_served_annually' uses homepage URL; deep link preferred

zakat.claim_evidence: The donation pages and 'About Us' sections of Hikma Health Inc. do not explicitly mention 'zakat,' 'zakat-compliant,' or 'zakat eligible' donations. There are no dedicated zakat funds or campaigns mentioned on their website. (Source: https://hikmahealth.org/donate)

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and specialized focus on global health technology, though its overall impact score is tempered by its role as a capacity builder rather than a direct service provider.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization aligns with global health urgency by addressing data gaps in refugee care [1]. While it is sadaqah-eligible, it represents a medium donor fit for those prioritizing technological innovation in humanitarian aid [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: Hikma Health demonstrates high financial health with a 100.0% program expense ratio and significant working capital of 26.0 months [1][2]. Its impact is categorized as capacity building, providing the digital infrastructure necessary for other nonprofits to serve underserved populations effectively [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Hikma Health Inc. empowers nonprofit healthcare providers in underserved regions through customized data systems and innovative health technology.
strengths: Maintains a 100.0% program expense ratio, directing all functional expenses toward its mission [2].
Strong liquidity with 26.0 months of working capital available to sustain operations [1].
High fundraising efficiency with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised [1].
baseline_narrative.summary: Hikma Health Inc. partners with global organizations to deploy specialized health and environmental record systems for refugee and vulnerable populations [1]. The organization reported a total revenue of $273,780 and maintains a focus on capacity building through its Care Innovation Grant and regional deployments [2].

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has <cite id="18">verified evidence and outcomes</cite> and a strong documented theory of change.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: By providing EHRs and data grants, Hikma Health enables local clinics to improve patient tracking and clinical outcomes.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that digital health tools actually lead to better patient care in the field.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="19">$273,780 funding gap</cite> and declining revenue, additional support is critical for scaling.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="4">100.0% program expense ratio</cite> and a fundraising efficiency of <cite id="9">$0.00 per $1 raised</cite>.
Hikma Health possesses <cite id="7">26.0 months of working capital</cite>, providing significant stability for long-term tech projects.
They provide <cite id="2">offline-ready EHR systems</cite> tailored for the unique needs of refugee populations.

strengths_deep_dive: Hikma Health's primary strength lies in its specialized delivery model, which focuses on <cite id="10">capacity building for nonprofit healthcare providers</cite>. By providing the digital infrastructure necessary for modern medicine, they enable local partners to track patient outcomes more effectively. Their financial health is bolstered by <cite id="11">total assets of $116,544</cite> against a modest expense base, ensuring that their <cite id="12">foreign grants</cite> and technical support can continue even during periods of fluctuating revenue.
rich_narrative.summary: Hikma Health Inc. is a technology-driven nonprofit that empowers healthcare providers in underserved regions by deploying <cite id="1">customized data systems and innovative health technology</cite>. The organization focuses on creating <cite id="2">offline-ready Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems</cite> specifically designed for refugee and vulnerable populations who often lack consistent internet access. In the 2023 fiscal year, Hikma Health reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $70,494</cite>, operating as a lean entity dedicated to capacity building. A defining characteristic of their financial model is a <cite id="4">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>, meaning every dollar of functional expense is directed toward their mission of health equity. Their technical portfolio includes the <cite id="5">Health and Environmental Record System (HERS)</cite> and a COVID-19 Policy Dataset, which assist clinicians in making data-driven decisions in resource-constrained environments like Nicaragua. Despite a <cite id="6">3-year revenue CAGR of -37.1%</cite>, the organization maintains exceptional liquidity, holding <cite id="7">26.0 months of working capital</cite> to sustain its ongoing software deployments and <cite id="8">Care Innovation Grant</cite> programs.
